frameworks.name: population-level coding
frameworks.one_line: Decoding stimulus identity from low-dimensional PFC population subspace

frameworks.name: stable mnemonic subspace
frameworks.one_line: Time-invariant subspace within high-dimensional state space supports WM

frameworks.name: heterogeneous single-neuron temporal dynamics
frameworks.one_line: Single-cell mixed selectivity contrasted with stable population code

frameworks.name: linear recurrent network model
frameworks.one_line: Proposed connectivity yielding stable population coding amid dynamics

core_question: How do PFC populations maintain stable working-memory representations despite heterogeneous single-neuron dynamics?
key_assumptions: Persistent activity supports parametric WM maintenance in PFC
Population state space contains task-relevant low-dimensional subspaces
manipulations: Compared stable vs time-optimized decoding subspaces
Two parametric WM tasks: oculomotor delayed response and vibrotactile delayed discrimination
Empirical data vs three candidate circuit models
key_findings: Low-dimensional PFC subspace carries stable stimulus code across cue and delay
Stable subspace decoding generalizes better than time-optimized subspaces
Single neurons show strong temporal heterogeneity despite stable population code
Three previously proposed circuit models fail to reproduce the observed population features
interpretations: PFC maintains robust WM via a stable population subspace coexisting with heterogeneous dynamics
Specific recurrent connectivity, captured by a linear network, can produce these stable-yet-dynamic features
